titleStmt.title: Joint Statement by President Roosevelt and the British Prime Minister ( (MacDonald))

div.head: Joint Statement by President Roosevelt and the British Prime Minister (MacDonald)Issued by the White House as a press release, April 22, 1933; reprinted from Department of State, Press Releases, April 22, 1933, p. 259.
div.p:
A preliminary discussion was held this forenoon between the President and the Prime Minister at which the following were present:
President Roosevelt
The Secretary of State
Assistant Secretary of State, Raymond Moley
Senator Key Pittman, of Nevada
Mr. Herbert Feis, Economic Advisor of the State Department
William C. Bullitt, Special Assistant to the Secretary of State
The Prime Minister
The British AmbassadorSir Ronald Charles Lindsay.
Sir Robert VansittartPermanent Undersecretary of State for Foreign Affairs.
Sir Frederick Leith-RossChief Economic Adviser to the British Government.
Mr. James BarlowPrincipal Private Secretary to the Prime Minister.
Mr. Arnold E. OvertonAssistant Secretary, Board of Trade.

The main problems of the World Economic Conference were reviewed and a decision was reached that these should be allocated in the first instance to the experts who would commence their discussions this afternoon and continue them tomorrow.
